questions.question: Which ancient Indian scripture is considered the earliest known poetic work in Sanskrit?
questions.explanation: The Rigveda, part of the Vedas, is the earliest known poetic work in Sanskrit, dating back to around 1500-1200 BCE.

questions.question: The Battle of Plassey was fought in which year?
questions.explanation: The Battle of Plassey was fought in 1757 and marked the beginning of British dominance in India.

questions.question: Who was the leader of the Non-Cooperation Movement launched in 1920?
questions.explanation: Mahatma Gandhi pioneered the Non-Cooperation Movement in 1920 as a non-violent protest against British rule.

questions.question: The 'Partition of Bengal' took place during the tenure of which Viceroy?
questions.explanation: The Partition of Bengal was carried out in 1905 during Lord Curzon's tenure to divide Bengal for administrative reasons.

questions.question: Who was the founder of the Indian National Congress?
questions.explanation: A.O. Hume is credited with founding the Indian National Congress in 1885.

questions.question: Which river is known as the 'Sorrow of Bihar' due to frequent floods?
questions.explanation: The Son River is called the 'Sorrow of Bihar' because of frequent devastating floods.

questions.question: Which is the largest Indian state in terms of area?
questions.explanation: Rajasthan is the largest Indian state by area, covering about 343,239 sq km.

questions.question: The Thar Desert is primarily located in which Indian state?
questions.explanation: The Thar Desert is mainly in Rajasthan.

questions.question: Which Indian city is famously called the 'Silicon Valley of India'?
questions.explanation: Bangalore is known as the 'Silicon Valley of India' due to its extensive IT industry.

questions.question: Who appoints the Chief Justice of India?
questions.explanation: The President of India appoints the Chief Justice of India.

questions.question: The ‘Directive Principles of State Policy’ are included in which part of the Indian Constitution?
questions.explanation: Directive Principles are included in Part IV of the Indian Constitution.

questions.question: Who is the head of the Panchayati Raj system in India?
questions.explanation: The Gram Sabha is the primary body of local self-governance in the Panchayati system.

questions.question: What is the tenure of the Lok Sabha elected in India?
questions.explanation: The Lok Sabha has a tenure of 5 years unless dissolved sooner.

questions.question: Which article of the Indian Constitution deals with the Fundamental Rights?
questions.explanation: Articles 14 to 32 of the Indian Constitution deal with Fundamental Rights.

questions.question: The 'Gross Domestic Product' (GDP) measures which of the following?
options: Total value of goods and services produced within a country
questions.explanation: GDP measures the total value of goods and services produced within a country.

questions.question: Which institution is responsible for banking regulation in India?
options: Securities and Exchange Board of India
questions.explanation: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) is the regulator of banking in India.

questions.question: The 'Five-Year Plans' in India were inspired by which economic model?
questions.explanation: India's Five-Year Plans were based on Keynesian economics focusing on state-led development.

questions.question: What does the 'Make in India' scheme primarily promote?
questions.explanation: 'Make in India' aims to promote manufacturing and attract investments in the sector.

questions.question: Which planet is known as the 'Red Planet'?
questions.explanation: Mars is called the 'Red Planet' because of its reddish appearance due to iron oxide.

questions.question: The chemical symbol 'Na' stands for which element?
questions.explanation: 'Na' is the chemical symbol for Sodium.

questions.question: Which part of the human body is primarily responsible for the production of insulin?
questions.explanation: The pancreas produces insulin, which regulates blood sugar levels.

questions.question: Which technology is used in satellites to provide global positioning services?
questions.explanation: GPS (Global Positioning System) uses satellites for navigation and positioning.

questions.question: Which of the following is a UNESCO World Heritage Site in India?
questions.explanation: The Taj Mahal is a UNESCO World Heritage Site in India.

questions.question: The Kyoto Protocol is associated with which global issue?
questions.explanation: The Kyoto Protocol is an international treaty aimed at reducing greenhouse gas emissions and combating climate change.

questions.question: Which of the following is a primary cause of acid rain?
questions.explanation: Sulfur dioxide and nitrogen oxides released from industrial processes cause acid rain.

questions.question: The term ‘Biodiversity Hotspot’ was first used by which organization?
questions.explanation: The term ‘Biodiversity Hotspot’ was introduced by Conservation International (ICUN) to identify regions with rich biodiversity under threat.

questions.question: Who is the current President of India as of July 2026?
questions.explanation: Droupadi Murmu is the current President of India since 25 July 2022.

questions.question: Who is the current Prime Minister of India?
questions.explanation: Narendra Modi has been serving as the Prime Minister since 26 May 2014.

questions.question: Who is the current Chief Minister of Odisha as of June 2024?
questions.explanation: Mohan Charan Majhi is the current Chief Minister of Odisha since 12 June 2024.

questions.question: Which country hosted the G20 Summit in 2023?
questions.explanation: India hosted the G20 Summit in 2023, with the theme 'One Earth, One Family, One Future'.
